steps.title: Identify the contrast structure
steps.content:
”And while outside the tempest is raving” signals a deliberate opposition.

The poem sets up two sharply different scenes: outside Puerto Cabello, a violent storm rages and ships are “madly driving” on dangerous shores.

Inside Puerto Cabello, the waters are “warm and land-locked,” moving with “easy motion,” and a canoe “gently glides.” This juxtaposition—danger versus safety, turbulence versus calm—is the organizing principle of the entire passage.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B directly names this structure: it states the main purpose is to contrast the tranquility of waters near Puerto Cabello with the roughness of waters elsewhere. That is exactly what the “while outside... thy waters” structure accomplishes.

Choice A focuses on attitude toward day-to-day life, which the text does not address. Choice C misreads “outside” as a future threat to the town itself, when the whole point is that Puerto Cabello is protected from the outside storm. Choice D reduces the passage to a mere boat comparison, missing the broader contrast in water conditions that the poem emphasizes.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
